metadata.title: Luxury Editorial Website Collection
metadata.subtitle: Apartamento × SSENSE × A24 Energy
metadata.description: A curated collection of 500+ websites embodying editorial restraint, typographic excellence, and atmospheric design

buckets.name: Typography-First / Editorial Minimal
buckets.description: Websites where custom typefaces are the primary visual element, with generous whitespace and grid-based layouts
filters: Custom or rare typefaces as primary visual element
Strict editorial hierarchy (H1 > H2 > body > caption)

sources.name: Sight Unseen
sources.description: Design and craft editorial by Jill Singer and Monica Khemsurov

sources.name: Remodelista
sources.description: Quiet design, sparse layout by Julie Carlson

sources.name: Apartment Therapy
sources.description: Accessible home culture by Maxwell Ryan

sources.name: Monocle
sources.description: Global affairs and design by Tyler Brûlé

sources.name: The Gentlewoman
sources.description: Grown-up fashion with clean editorial design

sources.name: Rollacoaster
sources.description: UK youth culture by Louise Gannon

sources.name: Flaunt Magazine
sources.description: LA fashion culture by Luis Barajas

sources.name: Nylon

sources.description: Youth fashion culture with bold design

sources.name: n+1
sources.description: Brooklyn literary journal with clean design

sources.name: Hyperallergic
sources.description: Sensitive to art and its discontents

buckets.name: Atmospheric / Cinematic (A24 Energy)
buckets.description: Immersive websites with film grain, analog textures, and mood over information hierarchy
filters: Grain, film noise, or analog texture overlays
Ambient audio or subtle sound design
Gothic, folk, or retro-futuristic aesthetic references

sources.name: A24
sources.description: The gold standard—atmospheric, immersive film studio

sources.name: Mubi
sources.description: Curated streaming with film journal aesthetic

sources.name: Film Comment
sources.description: Film Society of Lincoln Center publication

sources.name: Vinegar Syndrome
sources.description: Cult film restoration with editorial design

sources.name: Kino Lorber
sources.description: Art house and classic film distributor

sources.name: Imagine Entertainment
sources.description: Ron Howard and Brian Grazer company

buckets.name: Luxury Fashion E-Commerce
buckets.description: Editorial-first fashion retail where lookbooks drive navigation and storytelling maximizes over quick purchase
filters: Product photography shot on film or large-format digital
No visible UI chrome (borders, buttons minimized)
Hover states that reveal secondary imagery
Loaders/transitions that feel like scene changes
Mobile experience mirrors editorial app, not retail

sources.name: MatchesFashion
sources.description: Clean editorial layout with magazine-style storytelling

sources.name: Dover Street Market
sources.description: Comme des Garçons chaos meets editorial discipline

sources.name: Kith
sources.description: Streetwear elevated to editorial art direction

buckets.name: Art & Culture Institutions
buckets.description: Museums and galleries where collection databases become editorial experiences with calm, authoritative energy
filters: Collection databases treated as editorial experiences
High-contrast black/white or institutional color systems
Archival photography presented with contemporary layout
Donor/membership sections that don't break the aesthetic

sources.name: The Shed
sources.description: Hudson Yards culture center with flexible design

sources.name: The Menil Collection
sources.description: Houston's private museum with serene design

sources.name: Tate Modern
sources.description: Power Station conversion with industrial design

sources.name: Louvre
sources.description: World's largest museum with encyclopedic design

sources.name: Victoria and Albert Museum
sources.description: World's leading art and design museum

buckets.name: Independent Publishing / Magazine
buckets.description: Print-first publications translated to web with long-form text, issue-based navigation, and personal editorial voice
filters: Print-first design language translated to web

sources.name: Apartamento
sources.description: Everyday interiors magazine—the reference for warm minimalism

sources.name: MACK
sources.description: Art book publisher with editorial design

sources.name: Taschen
sources.description: Art book publisher with bold design

buckets.name: Creative Studios & Portfolios
buckets.description: Agencies and studios where work speaks first, with custom interactions and WebGL experiments
filters: Contact forms that feel like art projects

sources.name: Locomotive
sources.description: Montreal web studio with award-winning design

buckets.name: Design & Architecture
buckets.description: Architecture publications and design platforms with uniform grids, material palettes, and location-based navigation
filters: Project thumbnails as uniform grids (no masonry)

buckets.name: Photography & Visual Arts
buckets.description: Photography platforms and visual art marketplaces with full-screen viewers and archival respect
filters: Full-screen image viewer with keyboard navigation
EXIF data or technical details optional
Minimal UI that disappears on scroll

buckets.name: Music & Culture
buckets.description: Music platforms and culture publications with audio-first interactions and dark mode defaults
filters: Dark mode default (black or deep navy)

universal_filters.description: Filters that apply across all buckets for the Apartamento × SSENSE × A24 aesthetic
filters: No pop-ups or cookie banners that break immersion
Negative space treated as active design element
Loading states designed, not default browser
Mobile experience reimagined, not just responsive

venn_diagram.description: How to combine buckets to achieve specific energies

combinations.name: Apartamento Energy
combinations.description: Warm minimalism, everyday beauty, editorial restraint

combinations.name: SSENSE Energy
combinations.description: Dark luxury, avant-garde commerce, product as editorial

combinations.name: A24 Energy
combinations.description: Cinematic immersion, mood over information, world-building
